title: VLP Extraction from Fecal Samples
author: Frej Larsen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2qaqdse
materials: Thaw frozen sample in ice bucket

steps.title: Transfer up to 500 mg of...
steps.instruction: Transfer up to 500 mg of sample to centrifuge tube and add SM buffer to reach a final volume of 5 mL . Place centrifuge tube back in ice box

steps.title: Homogenize samples by placing ice box...
steps.instruction: Homogenize samples by placing ice box on shaking board at 300 rpm, °C, 10 minutes

steps.title: Filter the supernatant through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter the supernatant through a 0.45 um PES filter into a new centrifuge tube

steps.title: Transfer 2
steps.instruction: Transfer 2.5 mL of the sample to the outer chamber of the concentrator

steps.title: Centrifuge sample in the concentrator at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge sample in the concentrator at 1500 rcf, 4 °C, 20 minutes

steps.title: Pour off liquid from the inner chamber
steps.instruction: Pour off liquid from the inner chamber

steps.title: Repeat the three previous steps until...
steps.instruction: Repeat the three previous steps until all liquid from the centrifuge tube has passed through the concentrator and the there is no liquid left in the inner chamber

steps.title: Add 100 ul SM buffer to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 ul SM buffer to the inner chamber and centrifuge 1500 rcf, 4 °C, 2 minutes

steps.title: Leave in fridge to release VLPs...
steps.instruction: Leave in fridge to release VLPs from filter for at least 12 hours and at most for 1 week before proceeding with spotting or DNA/RNA extraction
